Author: Merisa Lee
Title: How to balance curiosity and AI for future engineers | Merisa Lee posted on the topic | LinkedIn
Text:
David Campbell’s recent post about the “coming engineering cliff” really resonated with me. I’ve talked before about how it feels like those coming up in the field today sometimes have less curiosity. Social media adds constant noise, and AI delivers quick answers that skip the hours of digging many of us once had to do. But as leaders, we have a responsibility: give people the chance to wrestle with tough issues. That’s how they build depth and resilience. Struggle is a teacher you can’t shortcut. At the same time, AI isn’t just a crutch — it’s a powerful tool. A quick Google search shows AI solving decades-old problems in medicine, science, and technology. That’s transformational. We’re at the cusp of a new revolution in technology. Gen X and Millennials saw personal computers come of age, the Internet, and cloud computing. Now it’s AI. The challenges Gen Z and Gen Alpha will solve won’t look like the ones we wrestled with in data centers at 3am. They’ll be different, bigger, and in many ways harder. Our job is to prepare them not by shielding them from hard problems — but by giving them the space and trust to solve them. What do you think? Are we helping the next generation of engineers stay curious while also equipping them to harness AI as a tool, not a crutch? #Leadership #Engineering #AI #Curiosity #CyberSecurity #FutureOfWork
When I joined Uber in 2015, on-call was survival. Dashboards bled red. Systems buckled under billions of requests. What I gained was not just technical skill, it was instinct. Those instincts, forged in fire, are what I call A+ engineers. And they are disappearing. Today, cloud abstractions and AI copilots make it easier than ever to build. But they also mean fewer engineers gain the scar tissue needed to keep billion-user systems alive. So here is the real question: Can AI learn the instincts that keep the internet from collapsing? I explore the “engineering cliff,” vibe coding, and whether AI can truly serve as an apprentice in my latest piece. Would love your thoughts. https://lnkd.in/gKbZnEcT
